channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Officials investigate blast at Tennessee explosives plant that left 18 missing and feared dead
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — Officials were investigating a blast that leveled an explosives plant in rural Tennessee, as families of the 18 people missing and feared dead waited anxiously Saturday for answers.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KREX 5

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: A food aid program that supports millions of low-income mothers and their young children received a $300 million infusion from the Trump administration this week, alleviating some anxiety that it would run out of money during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Maine Gov. Janet Mills will challenge Sen. Susan Collins in key 2026 Senate contest, AP sources say
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Maine’s two-term Democratic Gov. Janet Mills will run for the U.S. Senate seat held by veteran Republican Sen. Susan Collins next year, two people familiar with Mills’ plans said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cash App giving $100 to users through October. How to enter
item.description: CashApp is giving away $100 to thousands of users every Friday through Oct. 24, as part of its Cash App Pool sweepstakes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing mom found alive in forest as officials investigate ‘suspicious’ house fire
item.description: Britney Gard, 46, called 911 from a forest near Bainbridge, Indiana, ending a week-long search that began after her house caught fire under suspicious circumstances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Palisades Fire suspect made anti-Trump social media posts, blamed climate change for causing wildfires
item.description: The man who allegedly sparked the deadly Palisades Fire made anti-Trump posts on social media and frequently shared how global warming is causing climate change.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: National Guard troops are patrolling in Memphis for the first time, as part of President Donald Trump’s federal task force.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando And Sudhin Thanawala, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dodgers advance to NLCS, beat Phillies 2-1 on Kerkering's 11th-inning error
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Philadelphia Phillies and Los Angeles Dodgers didn't blink as the pressure built, inning after scoreless inning. Both teams pushed a single run across, forcing extra innings. Then came one of postseason baseball's craziest endings.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker formally nominates Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.), in a letter to members of the Norwegian Nobel Committee, said she felt Trump deserved the prize “in recognition of his bold and unprecedented leadership in advancing peace around the globe.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Political Power Ranker: Analyzing this year's biggest elections
item.description: On this week's edition of Political Power Ranker, we're breaking down some of the biggest races across the country.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Kiss rocker Gene Simmons recovering after crashing SUV
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Kiss rock star Gene Simmons is recovering after he fainted while driving along the Pacific Coast Highway in Malibu and crashed his SUV into a parked car, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Flight delayed or canceled during government shutdown? Here's what you should do
item.description: The federal government shutdown has entered its second week, and already shortages of air traffic controllers have strained operations and disrupted flights at some U.S. airports.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and RIO YAMAT AP Transportation and Airlines Writers, Fox 5

item.title: Texas National Guard troops now protecting federal property in Chicago area, official says
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A small number of National Guard troops has started protecting federal property in the Chicago area and assisting law enforcement in Memphis on Wednesday, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: New California law aims to improve school nutrition by phasing out some ultraprocessed foods
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California will phase out certain ultraprocessed foods from school meals over the next decade under a first-in-the-nation law signed Wednesday by Gov. Gavin Newsom.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Andrew Cuomo wants to save Rikers Island, New York's notorious jail complex
item.description: New York City mayoral candidate Andrew Cuomo says he wants to save Rikers Island, the notorious jail complex that has become a symbol of dysfunction in the city's criminal justice system.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman sues haunted house after breaking both ankles
item.description: A Nebraska woman says a jump meant to thrill visitors at a haunted house in Kansas City, Missouri, left her with two broken ankles.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: The shutdown is already causing flight delays. Service to more than 170 airports could stop entirely
item.description: Several major airports were experiencing delays on Wednesday, and smaller airports were at risk of losing service altogether by the end of the week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Pentagon, journalists on collision course as restrictions on reporting loom
item.description: Facing a deadline next week on whether to sign a statement acknowledging new restrictions on how they do their jobs or risk being thrown out of the Pentagon, journalists who cover the U.S. military appear headed toward a showdown with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The shutdown is already causing flight delays. Service to more than 170 airports could stop entirely
item.description: Several major airports were experiencing delays on Wednesday, and smaller airports were at risk of losing service altogether by the end of the week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says Illinois governor and Chicago mayor should be jailed as they oppose Guard deployment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the Illinois governor and Chicago mayor should be jailed as they oppose his deployment of National Guard troops for his immigration and crime crackdown in the nation's third-largest city. The officials said they would not be deterred.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Changes to SNAP benefits take effect in October: What to know
item.description: The federal government may be shut down, but that didn't stop changes to SNAP benefits from kicking in on the first of the month, which is also the start of the fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Manchester synagogue attacker pledged allegiance to Islamic State group, police say
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The assailant in last week’s attack on a synagogue in the northwest of England that left two congregants dead pledged allegiance to the Islamic State group, police said Wednesday. The attacker, Jihad Al-Shamie, called emergency dispatchers during his deadly attack on Oct. 2.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Divorce rates 'significantly higher' in these states, Census Bureau says
item.description: Between 2012 to 2022, the divorce rate declined 28%, according to the U.S. Census Bureau. However, there are 14 states bucking the trend, where the divorce rate is still "significantly higher" than the national average.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Your phone rings, and it's a number from Sweden. Do you answer? A Nobel Prize winner didn't
item.description: This year’s Nobel Prize science winners got the good news from early morning phone calls and door knocks.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio And Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Flight delays spread to major airports across US
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) reported shortages at airports in Nashville, Tenn., Boston, Dallas, Chicago and Philadelphia, as well as at air traffic control centers in Atlanta, Houston and the Dallas-Fort Worth area.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, Fox 5

item.title: 1 in 5 people in US is Latino for first time in history: Analysis
item.description: For the first time in American history, one in five people living in the U.S. identify as Latino, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of hikers rescued from Mount Everest after severe snowstorm
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — About 900 hikers, guides and other staff who were stranded by a weekend snowstorm on the Chinese side of Mount Everest have reached safety, state media said late Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
